signals.claim: Internship postings on Handshake declined by more than 15 percent while application activity increased; 41 percent of Class of 2025 students had applied to at least one internship through Handshake by January 2025, versus 34 percent of the Class of 2023 by graduation.
signals.content_implication: Treat competition, timing, and application quality as separate student problems rather than promising that volume alone fixes outcomes.

signals.claim: Handshake reported a 15 percent year-over-year decline in job postings and a 30 percent increase in applications per job for the Class of 2025.
signals.content_implication: Opportunity-radar posts should show fit, freshness, and alternatives rather than only announcing a crowded opening.

signals.claim: Handshake identifies earlier recruiting, changing AI expectations, and repeated employer familiarity as three structural shifts in the 2026 internship market.
signals.content_implication: Build recurring early-calendar, AI-boundary, and employer-familiarity series instead of one-off seasonal posts.

signals.claim: NACE reported that 64.8 percent of surveyed employers use skills-based hiring practices for new entry-level hires.
signals.content_implication: Keep Close the Proof Gap as the flagship series and organize it around demonstrable skills rather than credential inflation.

signals.claim: For people age 16 to 24, BLS reported unemployment rates of 7.4 percent for some college or an associate degree and 7.0 percent for a bachelor's degree or higher.
signals.content_implication: Avoid framing difficult early-career searches as individual failure; distinguish market context from controllable application steps.

signals.claim: The FTC warns college students about fake job ads and recruiter messages, payment demands for training or starter kits, and fake-check requests that ask the student to forward money.
signals.content_implication: Student Scam Check should be a recurring first-class series with an official-source verification step.

signals.claim: USCIS distinguishes CPT, OPT, and STEM OPT; CPT requires authorization from the student's designated school official and must be integral to an established curriculum.
signals.content_implication: International-student posts must link to official guidance, direct students to their DSO, and avoid individualized legal advice.

signals.claim: The Department of Labor describes a flexible seven-factor primary-beneficiary test for determining whether an intern at a for-profit employer is an employee entitled to minimum wage and overtime.
signals.content_implication: Unpaid-internship content must explain the factors and uncertainty instead of declaring legality from a single detail.

signals.claim: Careerflow positions resume building, ATS checks, LinkedIn optimization, job tracking, mock interviews, and cover letters as one student job-search suite.
signals.content_implication: AI assistance is a crowded claim; Internships.com needs a clearer evidence-truthfulness and human-review distinction.

signals.claim: Big Interview presents a learn, practice, and feedback loop using expert lessons, realistic questions, and AI-supported coaching.
signals.content_implication: Interview content should demonstrate repeatable practice and reflection, not perfect-answer scripts.

signals.claim: 12twenty positions itself as campus-first infrastructure spanning career-center operations, outcome reporting, experiential learning, job boards, interviews, and career fairs.
signals.content_implication: Institutional workflow and outcome data belong in a separate B2B2C intelligence lane rather than student editorial content.

signals.claim: Riipen positions real business and nonprofit projects as a way for students to gain work-ready experience and grow professional networks.
signals.content_implication: Proof-building alternatives deserve their own comparison lane alongside job-board discovery.

measurement_gaps: Google Trends returned HTTP 429 during the current US query attempt; no relative-demand values are asserted.
Company product counts and outcome claims remain company-reported marketing claims unless separately verified.
The public web pass does not substitute for the credential-blocked social profile and search collection lanes.
